notes: Sanitised public contaminant monitoring bundle retains governed signoff state, legal-limit posture, and non-confidential review resources only.
Confidential fields and resources are represented in sanitisation_records rather than exposed directly.
Contaminant monitoring import checks validate supplied CSV text against governed occurrence and analytical-method evidence objects.
This workflow is review-oriented and does not create a native contaminant exposure engine or final regulatory decision.

Occurrence-evidence record eu.mercury.occurrence_evidence.official_monitoring_context: Supporting sources mix historical anchors (efsa.mercury.food.2012) with newer context records (efsa.mercury.fish_consumption.2026, efsa.metals.topic.2026, eu.reg.2023_915, eu.reg.333_2007); confirm the older evidence base is still fit for the intended use.

Occurrence-evidence record eu.mercury.occurrence_evidence.official_monitoring_context still depends on older supporting sources (efsa.mercury.food.2012) even though newer context records are also linked.

Analytical-method-evidence record eu.mercury.analytical_method_evidence.official_control: Supporting sources mix historical anchors (efsa.mercury.food.2012) with newer context records (eu.reg.2023_915, eu.reg.333_2007); confirm the older evidence base is still fit for the intended use.

Analytical-method-evidence record eu.mercury.analytical_method_evidence.official_control still depends on older supporting sources (efsa.mercury.food.2012) even though newer context records are also linked.
Broad-food-supply fallback matching was used for at least one evidence record; treat the matrix linkage as review support rather than a matrix-specific confirmation.
Interpretation bundle packages the contaminant monitoring check, governed evidence records, and reviewer prompts into one audit-ready object.
Bundle remains review-oriented and does not convert contaminant monitoring context into a native exposure or final decision engine.
Attached legal-limit review snapshots make exact, partial, anchor-only, or missing jurisdiction support explicit instead of implying a complete legal-limit layer.
EU does not currently ship curated contaminant legal-limit coverage for matrix group `broad_food_supply`.
EU does not currently ship curated contaminant legal-limit coverage for matrix group `fish_and_seafood`.
EU does not currently ship curated contaminant legal-limit coverage for matrix group `large_predatory_fish`.
Signoff packet is a reviewer-facing overlay on top of the contaminant monitoring interpretation bundle and does not change the underlying bundle content.
Waived actions remain explicitly visible so downstream reviewers can inspect accepted deviations from the default contaminant monitoring review sequence.
Signed-off status records closure of the governed review workflow only; it does not certify scientific correctness, submission readiness, or regulatory approval.
Attached legal-limit review snapshots remain visible in signoff so partial, anchor-only, or missing jurisdiction support cannot be mistaken for a complete legal-limit layer.
At least one blocking contaminant monitoring action remains unresolved in the signoff packet.

sourceGovernanceSnapshot.title: Frequency of consumption of different species of fish, crustaceans and molluscs and the effectiveness of national advice in relation to mercury contamination
notes: EFSA published this official annex on 12 February 2026 covering the frequency of consumption of different fish, crustacean, and mollusc species and the effectiveness of national advice related to mercury contamination.
Used in Dietary MCP as current official context for species-sensitive fish and seafood review, especially for high consumers and pregnancy-related advisory questions.

sourceGovernanceSnapshot.title: Scientific Opinion on the risk for public health related to the presence of mercury and methylmercury in food
notes: EFSA adopted the mercury and methylmercury opinion on 22 November 2012 and published it on 20 December 2012.
The opinion established a TWI of 1.3 micrograms per kilogram body weight per week for methylmercury and 4 micrograms per kilogram body weight per week for inorganic mercury.

sourceGovernanceSnapshot.title: Metals as contaminants in food
notes: Current EFSA topic page summarising risk assessments and legal context for arsenic, cadmium, lead, mercury and other metals as contaminants in food.
Used in Dietary MCP as a maintained official context source for metals-contaminant governance.

sourceGovernanceSnapshot.title: Commission Regulation (EU) 2023/915 on maximum levels for certain contaminants in food
notes: Binding EU contaminant regulation that includes maximum levels for trace elements, PFAS, and other contaminants in food.
Used in Dietary MCP as a shared EU legal anchor for PFAS, cadmium, lead, and mercury food-contaminant governance.

sourceGovernanceSnapshot.title: Commission Regulation (EC) No 333/2007 laying down the methods of sampling and analysis for the official control of the levels of trace elements and processing contaminants in foodstuffs
notes: Current consolidated EU official-control regulation covering sampling and analysis requirements for trace elements and processing contaminants in foodstuffs.
Used in Dietary MCP as the shared official-control source anchor for cadmium, lead, inorganic arsenic, and mercury food-contaminant governance.

emergingContaminantSnapshot.displayName: Mercury food contaminants and dietary exposure watchlist
notes: This family is exposed as a governed mercury food contaminants and dietary exposure watchlist with explicit EFSA and EU legal provenance.
Submission-oriented use is allowed when the dossier meets standard governance and fingerprint requirements.

sanitisationRecords.note: Confidential pinned resources are omitted from sanitised public dossiers.

limitations.message: This dossier is version-pinned through release and resource fingerprints but is not cryptographically signed in v0.1.
This dossier records contaminant monitoring review and escalation posture only and does not represent a native exposure engine or final regulatory decision package.
EU does not currently ship curated contaminant legal-limit coverage for matrix group `broad_food_supply`. No cross-jurisdiction or cross-matrix borrowing is implied.
EU does not currently ship curated contaminant legal-limit coverage for matrix group `fish_and_seafood`. No cross-jurisdiction or cross-matrix borrowing is implied.
EU does not currently ship curated contaminant legal-limit coverage for matrix group `large_predatory_fish`. No cross-jurisdiction or cross-matrix borrowing is implied.
Sanitised public dossier omits confidential fields and resources and should not be treated as a complete internal review record.

notes: Sanitised public dossier derived from a version-pinned internal review dossier.
Retained provenance and pinned fingerprints remain machine-readable for public review of non-confidential content.
Sanitised public contaminant monitoring dossier preserves legal-limit review posture so partial, anchor-only, or missing jurisdiction support remains explicit in non-confidential exchange.
Version-pinned dossier captures the exact release metadata hashes and governed contaminant-monitoring manifests used during review.
Escalation items are derived only from explicit waivers and unresolved blocking actions recorded in the signoff packet.

This dossier captures a governed review state and escalation posture only; it does not certify scientific correctness, submission readiness, or a submission-capable contaminant monitoring decision package.
Family- and jurisdiction-scoped contaminant legal-limit payloads are pinned so exact, partial, anchor-only, or missing support remains explicit in downstream review.
At least one reviewer waiver or unresolved blocking action remains visible in the dossier escalation overlay.
